objective: Maximize persistence skill of a 72h z500/t850 WeatherBench-2 forecast model built under a fixed compute budget
metric: persistence skill score (1 - mean(rmse/persistence); >0 beats persistence)

criteria.outcome: model.py exists and defines train_and_predict(Xtr,Ytr,Xte,meta,time_budget_s,device) that runs end-to-end and returns finite predictions of correct shape.
criteria.observe: Read model.py; run/submit it and confirm no errors, output is finite (np.isfinite(pred).all()) with shape matching Xte.

criteria.outcome: The model predicts the residual (Y-X) and normalizes inputs using meta['mean']/meta['std'], with circular (periodic) padding on the longitude axis.
criteria.observe: Inspect model.py source for residual target (Y-X, add X back at inference), mean/std normalization, and circular padding on lon dim.

criteria.outcome: Training respects the compute budget: it monitors time.time() and stops before time_budget_s without overrunning.
criteria.observe: Read model.py for a time.time() deadline check in the training loop; confirm submission run does not exceed time_budget_s.

criteria.outcome: Achieved persistence skill score > 0 on the official submission (beats persistence baseline).
criteria.observe: Submit the model and read the reported skill score; confirm 1 - mean(rmse/persistence) > 0.

criteria.outcome: The best submitted model's skill is reported clearly and reflects iterative improvement over the first working submission.
criteria.observe: Compare skill across submissions; confirm final/best skill >= first working skill and is stated explicitly.
